data.title: 2022 Year in Review￼
data.content:
In 2022, SIMAD University continued advancing its ever-growing academic and human-centered success legacy. For the 5th time in a row, SIMAD University remains the leading university in Somalia according to Webometrics Ranking of World Universities. The university remains the nation’s leading research university in Somalia, publishing 64 citation index papers in 2022 and having over 30 papers under review.

This ending year is filled with achievements for SIMAD University, from unveiling a new campus at the heart of Mogadishu to the launch of the largest fully-fledged innovation hub in Somalia by H.E. Hassan Sheikh Mohamud to the installation of node chairs and ViewSonic’s smartboard interactive whiteboard across the new town campus to undergoing a Transformation Project of preparing SIMAD into becoming an entrepreneurial university. For SIMAD, 2022 was brilliant indeed.

Below is the SIMAD 2022 Year in Review, which summarizes our accomplishments.

Academics & HR Success

  • SIMAD launched Cambridge Lab, the first official agent to offer Cambridge’s LinguaSkill. Like other official tests, the LinguaSkill from Cambridge assesses all English skills, including speaking, listening, reading, and writing. The test report provides CEFR level (A1-C1) and is widely used by employers, educational institutions, and government entities to prove an individual’s English level.
  • SIMAD University, part of the Direct-Aid Sponsored Universities Consortium (DASUC), is one of the leading universities that will undergo a Transformation Project to prepare SIMAD University to become an entrepreneurial university. This is part of revising the university’s core operations to contribute to Somalia’s economic and societal development.
  • For the 5th time in a row, SIMAD University has been ranked as the number one (1) university in Somalia according to the latest edition of Webometrics Ranking of World Universities released in June 2022.
  • The university conducted Faculty Development Initiative, a program designed to boost faculty skills and performance in research and teaching through productive workshop training sessions.
  • In response to the changing nature and demands of the local labor market, SIMAD University introduced new programs, including digital marketing, entrepreneurship and innovation, taxation, auditing, biostatistics, and digital radiography.
  • As part of human capital development, the University senate awarded postgraduate scholarships to 14 academic staff (4 PhDs and 10 Masters).
  • In collaboration with Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM), SIMAD University Dhoobaale Library organized a collaborative webinar training on SLiMS, an open-source library management system, for library staff and some selected students from the Faculty of Computing. Dr. Mohamad Rahimi from UiTM conducted the webinar. The purpose of the webinar training was to enhance the library automation skill of library staff and university students.
  • The university’s academic council paid for a study tour to Rwanda, where they met and discussed with potential partners, including the University of Rwanda, Fablab Rwanda, and ICT experts.
  • Our Faculty of Engineering has initiated a Chess Club to promote the students’ intellectual and cognitive skills, including critical thinking, problem-solving, pattern recognition, and concentration. The Somali Chess Federation officially allowed the club to join the federation and pledged its support in providing chess sets and beyond. 
  • Our Dean of the Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences, Dr-Najib Isse Dirie, was invited to serve as an external examiner for the end-cycle examination of medical students in the undergraduate program in the School of Medicine and Pharmacy at the College of Medicine and Health Sciences, University of Rwanda.
  • The university modernized its teaching materials at Town Campus by replacing traditional chairs with Node Chairs. This complete chair can move 360 degrees to facilitate the learning process for every student. The university also replaced traditional boards with smart and interactive ViewSonic boards.
  • The university organized this year Computing Week and Engineering Week. During the two weeks, our Faculty of Computing and Faculty of Engineering hosted workshops, project demonstrations, and panel discussions to build and improve our students’ quality skills.
  • The 3rd batch of medical students went to Rwanda to receive medical training at the University of Rwanda Hospital for a year.
  • SIMAD University Dhoobaale Library organized literate programs to impact students by focusing on reading courses related materials but also to help them widen their capacity and stimulus to know more diverse knowledge across the universe through the available materials.
  • Our Graduate Studies introduced a new program- Master of Information Technology- as an addition to the programs in collaboration with Open University Malaysia. The program aims to produce responsible, innovative, and creative graduates with skills in planning, organizing, designing, and managing various ICT-based solutions for different organizations.
  • SIMAD University Dhoobaale Library is now an affiliate member of the International Federation of Library Associations and Institutions (IFLA), the global voice of the library and information profession.

Community Engagement & Social Events

  • As part of social responsibility, SIMAD University students led by the Student Government (XAJSi) gave contributions to the President of the Association of Somali Universities. These contributions, collected by students, are intended to support drought relief in the country.
  • SIMAD Town Campus and SIMAD iLab were officially unveiled by President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud in a grand opening ceremony held at the town campus.
  • SIMAD University Dr. Sumait Hospitals celebrated its 3rd anniversary on January 22 to reflect the significant achievements and recognition of clinical outcomes and patient safety for the past three years.
  • In an attempt to engage the community, potential students from secondary schools attended a career mapping program held at the main campus. They received a comprehensive workshop that empowered them to identify postsecondary goals, explore university and career options, and develop skills necessary for university success.
  • Attended the 54th Conference of the Association of Arab Universities. The Rector invited the Secretary General of the Association of Arab Universities, Prof. Dr. Amr Ezzat Salama, to visit Somalia and inspect the country’s higher education.
  • Hosted annual conferences, including CEST, CMHS, CEPAD, and CEB.
  • Hosted the Annual Iftar Gathering.
  • In commemorating Somalia’s National Tree Planting Day, SIMAD Family, led by the Rector and Social Science Students, planted around 40 trees at SIMAD Town Campus.
  • The annual cultural week event has been successfully conducted. It is a week-long cultural festival intended to inspire our students to show the diversity and richness of our Somali culture.
  • On November 6, 2022, SIMAD University celebrated its 23rd anniversary of the University.
  • The University participated in the United Nations Conference of the Parties (COP27) represented by our own Mohamed Okash in Sharm El Sheikh, Egypt. SIMAD University was among a handful of universities across the world gathered at the COP27 to advance innovative solutions towards achieving the Paris agreement and conventions in Somalia and beyond.
  • Dr. Awale Abdullahi of SIMAD University Dr. Sumait Hospitals participated in the 22nd College of Surgeons of East Central and Southern Africa (COSECSA) AGM, Graduation, and Scientific Conference in Windhoek, Namibia. COSECSA is a non-profit making professional body that fosters postgraduate education in surgery and provides surgical training throughout the East, Central, and Southern African regions. The theme for this year’s conference was “Timely Response to Current and Emerging Surgical Challenges on the Continent.” Dr. Awale presented a study titled ‘foreign body removal at high-risk areas in the body at selected hospitals: A retrospective study.’
  • Our Faculty of Law held a Moot Court attended by students from the Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences. This court, under collaboration between the Faculty of Law and Medicine, is the first of its kind, aiming to integrate the various disciplines offered by the University. The purpose of this year’s moot court was to alert future doctors and health professionals to the crimes and consequences of patient abuse and neglect.
  • Our Faculty of Medicine & Health Sciences, Department of Medical Imaging Technology, joined the rest of the world in celebrating World Radiography Day under the theme ‘Advocating for Radiation Protection Act.”

PARTNERSHIPS & ENGAGEMENT

  • SIMAD signed an agreement with Ednastics, an education consultancy, content distributor, and an approved agent for Cambridge Assessment English. The agreement allows the university to host LinguaSkill Cambridge Assessment English, a convenient English test to help organizations and universities check the English levels of individuals and groups of candidates.
  • SIMAD signed agreements with several local universities, including
    • Zamzam University of Science and Technology
    • Abrar University
    • University of Bosaso
    • University of Health Sciences
  • SIMAD signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Idman Community Bank. This agreement aims to establish a general framework to promote collaboration between the Bank and the university in matters of common interest.
  • SIMAD is now licensed to run ACCA Computer-Based Exams (CBE). As an authorized ACCA CBE center, we accept ACCA students wishing to sit computer-based examinations for timely and on-demand ACCA exams using our fully equipped computer lab at SIMAD Town Campus.
  • Delegates from SIMAD paid a visit to Gaziantep University and Karadeniz Technical University in Turkey under the framework of staff mobility of the Erasmus+ KA107 program. The delegates discussed with their counterparts a possible collaboration on areas of mutual interest.
  • Attended the Annual Meeting Davos2022 of the World Economic Forum. This year’s Annual Meeting came at a critical period when the world faces simultaneous crises, including climate change, conflicts, and food & energy crises. Our rep, Okash, was among over 2500 leaders & experts coming across the world to forge formidable solutions.
  • The management of SIMAD University virtually met the leadership of South-Central College headed by the Rector, Dahir Hassan, and the President, Dr. Annette Parker, respectively. The administrations of both institutions have discussed potential areas of collaboration and partnership. An Ad-Hoc committee has been formed to work on the details of the engagement and partnership. To take this initiative forward, Dr. Parker also invited SIMAD to visit South Central College.
  • Delegates from SIMAD paid a courtesy visit to Makerere University, Gulu University, and Kampala International University to foster partnerships and greater cooperation between SIMAD and these institutions.
  • SIMAD has signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the University of Bayreuth in Germany. Both universities will collaborate in various activities on education, constitutional law, legal frameworks, climate, and environmental protection.
  • SIMAD has signed a memorandum of understanding with Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM). This agreement allows students who graduate from SIMAD University’s Faculty of Medicine to do higher specializations at UKM University. The agreement also allows SIMAD medical students to undergo medical training at UKM University Hospital in their final academic year from 2023.
  • Zamzam University of Science and Technology, SIMAD University, and Jamhuriya University of Science and Technology signed a research collaboration agreement. In alignment with the previous research cooperation between SIMAD University and Jamhuria University, Zamzam University will be part of the Conference on Engineering, Science, and Technology.
  • Delegates led by The President of the University of Jigjiga, Dr. Bashir Abdullahi, paid a courtesy visit to SIMAD University Town Campus to exchange opinions and information and work towards continuing and strengthening the cooperation between the two institutions.

INNOVATION AND RESEARCH

  • Together with the International Trade Centre (ITC), the joint agency of the World Trade Organization and the United Nations, SIMAD iLab joined forces to launch the Digital Skills for Jobs project in Mogadishu, funded by the Government of Japan. Almost 80% of the trainees have secured either job, freelancing offer, or created a digital startup with an income generation of almost 50,000 USD in about 100 days.
  • The Director of SIMAD Innovation Lab toured the innovation ecosystem in Europe. The Director also met with partners in Brussels and Amsterdam. SIMAD iLab is recently selected by UNIDO Somalia as one of the implementing partners for an upcoming project for business incubators in Somalia. The goal is to transform the hub as one of the innovation champions in the country.
  • To contribute to a paperless, green university, the ICT office has continued automating the various operations of the university, including the Graduate School, SIMAD Innovation Lab, student financial documents, and others.
  • SIMAD maintained its position as a leading research university in Somalia; about 64 citation index papers were published in 2022, and over 30 papers are currently under review.
  • Launched the first SIMAD University’s Research Fellowship Program to empower SIMAD staff to conduct research and publish in an indexed journal. Also, SIMAD Journal Club was introduced as a platform for the research cluster and research fellow members to exchange research expertise and address key contextualized research challenges.
  • A team from Dr. Sumait Hospitals and the Faculty of Medicine and Health Sciences participated in the 1st NIH Health Research held in Garowe, organized by the Ministry of Health- Somalia and the National Institute of Health. Our team contributed to the 1st NIH Research Conference by presenting their latest research findings.
  • In collaboration with the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM), SIMAD University received a research grant to jointly conduct a study to inform pandemic response and recovery of emergency-affected populations by determining public health needs, improving methods, and integrating services to mitigate morbidity and mortality.
  • A team of researchers has investigated changes in extreme rainfall trends for two phases (1901–1958 and 1959–2016) across Somalia. They found some exciting shifts in precipitation extremes in the past century and that the extreme rainfall intensity and frequency have increased significantly over Somalia. They suggest the rigorous need for new country-level contingency planning, as the current situation demonstrates more intense and regular precipitation extremes.
  • Dr-Najib Isse Dirie, Dean, Faculty of Medicine and Health Science, published a rare case in the Urology Case Reports Journal of Science Direct. Transverse Testicular Ectopia with Persistent Mullerian Duct Syndrome is a rare case and was estimated to occur in 1 in 8 Million Males. This is highly unique and has never been reported from the whole region of Sub-saharan Africa. In line with this, Dr-Najib Isse Dirie was a guest speaker at a continuous medical education workshop organized by the University of Rwanda Teaching Hospital of Butare (CHUB) to present his latest research finding.
  • The Center for Research and Development Director went to the International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI), Ethiopia Campus, for a three-day workshop and training event for research coordinators from designated universities in the Horn of Africa. The workshop aims to deepen participants’ understanding of One Health principles and approaches and to discuss ways to support One Health Multidisciplinary Research.

SIMAD Visits & Tours

  • Hosted delegates from UNDP led by Jennifer Colville, Manager of UNDP’s Regional Innovation Portfolio in Arab states. The University’s top management received the team, briefed them about the University, and toured SIMAD iLab. The delegates appreciated the tremendous contributions of the University in Somalia and promised further engagement and collaboration.
  • Hosted Ambassador Salah Elguneid, the Head of Mission of The Arab States in Somalia. The university welcomed the Ambassador and toured him around SIMAD University. The Ambassador appreciated the tremendous contributions of the University in Somalia.
  • Hosted delegates from the International Trade Centre. Through SIMAD Innovation Lab, the university completed the #DigitalSkills4Jobs program, which trained the Somali youth in digital entrepreneurship and freelancing.

That’s a wrap! We have had an exceptional and extraordinary year, which would not have been possible without our exceptional staff and students. Thank you to everyone in our family and community who contributed to this impressive list of accomplishments over 2022.

data.title: Orientation Week for Class of 2026 | Program Schedule

data.title: SIMAD University Celebrates its 21st Anniversary
data.content:
A beautiful ceremony to mark the 21st anniversary of SIMAD University was held at the SIMAD University main campus. The event was attended by SIMAD senior management, Direct Aid Director, SIMAD University professors, graduates, parents, students, and other dignitaries. They all came together to attend, celebrate, and reflect on the achievements of the University. The University is very proud and happy to be 21 years old.

The university was established as a higher education institution on November 6, 1999, and later in 2011 was transformed into a fully functioning university. During that time, the University has made tremendous growth and progress and is still moving forward to achieve more.

Every year, SIMAD University celebrates SIMAD DAY, the day SIMAD was founded. Today marks the 21st anniversary of SIMAD University. We have all come together to reflect on the success and performance of the university. After the ceremony, top Management gives their speech.

 Ustad Yusuf Moalim Mohamed, the Senior Adviser to the Rector, reminds us how SIMAD has made to come here, advised the current generations, and said “There was a time that this campus was having only one building and only two faculties and know you are all witness on where we are. SIMAD has passed the struggle of evacuation time that cannon strikes around us. I’m advising this generation to strive to proceed with the progress and I have a wish of success to all of you” Also Eng. Mohamed Mohamud Mohamed, the Deputy, and Acting Rector, peaks the SIMAD values, and praised the 2020 award winners “Greetings to all and Happy SIMADDAY. SIMAD has created a Culture of Voluntary and social work and Innovation. It’s not money that makes great SIMAD, but the people who learn from it and work for it. Everyone in SIMAD deserves Award, but we are praising those who got the Awards this year. A bright future is always ahead and we are willing to achieve more”

Eventually, Sheikh Hassan Ahmed Badawi, the Director of Direct-AID has concluded the speech with this “I’m Congratulating to SIMAD for its anniversary of 21 years. We always talk in every gathering time, the time of struggle that we have struggled to pass and we realize that hard time in hard work pays off, as we have seen today we have people who led this nation and makes great contributions. Now We have to think about where we are heading. Appreciation to people who bring SIMAD here, the former Deans such as one of its founding fathers H.E Hassan Sheikh Mohamud, and I have great appreciation to the current Rector Ustad. Dahir Hassan, for the time and efforts he put in order to have lands and buildings to this Institution”

After their speech, they presented the awards to those has been recognized they deserve. Here in the List Below, are the 2020 Award recipients

data.title: XAJSi INAUGURATION CEREMONY
data.content:
The inauguration ceremony for the recently-elected Student Government (XAJSi) was held today at Safari Hotel in Mogadishu. The top management of the University including the Acting Rector, Eng. Mohamed Mohamud, faculty members, lecturers and students attended the ceremony.

Opening the ceremony, Dean of Students Affairs, Mr. Ali Ibrahim assured his support for the activities of the Student Government and urged them to strive for the realization of XAJSi goals.

The outgoing Student Government President, Mr. Abdikarim Abdullahi Mohamed, reflected his previous experience in the Student Government. It had been an inspirational and insightful experience for him and his members. He appreciated the support of the Rector, Faculty, Students Affairs Office, and students during his tenure and thanked SIMAD University for giving them the opportunity to be involved in XAJSi and University events.

“We developed a plan last year, a plan that would provide the best learning experience and environment to the students, unfortunately, with the COVID-19 pandemic that resulted in the closure of schools and colleges, some of our plans could not get executed. I am glad that my successor will now be taking the lead and hopefully he will continue implementing all these plans along with his own agendas”

Mr. Abdikarim said.

Continuing the ceremony, the incoming President of the Student Government, Mr. Mohamed Abdi Hassan addressed the audience, expressing his gratitude to the students for choosing him as the rightful candidate. Mr. Mohamed promised that his government will deliver their best during the year and assured that they will continue the efforts and pending activities of the previous government members.

Finally, the Acting Rector of SIMAD University, Eng. Mohamed Mohamud Mohmed, closing the ceremony, appreciated the efforts and hard work of the outgoing student government and congratulated the incoming student government executives and wished them a good tenure.

At SIMAD, Student Government was first established during the academic year of 2013-2014, with an objective to give students a platform to showcase their talents and help them develop their career, personality, and organizational skills through various interactive programs. This incoming government becomes the 7th Student Government to serve and represent the interests of undergraduate students.

data.title: International University of Africa held a farewell ceremony for SU medical students
data.content:
International University of Africa (IUA) held a farewell session for our first batch of medical students who are now returning home after spending one semester at IUA campus. Despite the academic activities, these students made new friends and learned more about the local culture while sharing theirs. It is about encouraging the development of academic and cultural exchanges between our two institutions.

We want to thank the IUA family and in particular to the Vice-Chancellor, Professor Kamal Ubaid for this great opportunity. We appreciate your kind hospitality, guidance and inspiration to our students during their stay at IUA campus.

Again, we want to thank everyone who made this program possible including the DVC academics Professor Musa Daha, the dean of the faculty of medicine Dr. Omar Aladil, and the all professors who helped our students tirelessly. This is the end of a great experience for our students in Khartoum, Sudan. They have good memories that now have a place in their hearts.

data.title: First Convocation of SIMAD Graduate studies
data.content:
A highly organized ceremony for 114 students who graduated from SIMAD Graduate studies was held in Hotel Jazeera, on Thursday 22nd November 2018.

Representatives from the Ministry of Education, SIMAD’s administration, students, parents, and other honorable guests have participated at the graduation ceremony.
Dean, SIMAD Graduate Studies, Dr. Amina Sheikh Omar has officially opened the ceremony and related the history of SIMAD Graduate studies and how it came into existence.

SU Rector Ustad Dahir Hassan Arab who addressed at the ceremony has sent congratulations to the all graduating students and their parents for their achievement, The Rector has announced Two Ph.D. scholarships to the outstanding students among the graduates.

Former interior minister of Somalia, Former Rector of SIMAD and current Member of Somali federal Parliament Mr. Abdirahman Mohamed Hussein (Odawara) has also addressed at the ceremony and prolonged advice to the graduates.

The State Minister for Education, Culture and Higher Education, H.E. Abdirahman Mohamud Abdulle (Jabiri) who concluded the ceremony has appreciated the SIMAD’s significant knowledge contribution to the Somali community.

SIMAD University has started a master’s degree in 2012 in collaboration with Open University Malaysia. However, in 2016 SIMAD has introduced SIMAD Graduate studies which provides Master courses in different disciplines.

data.title: SIMAD Hosts the Second Medical Conference
data.content:
SIMAD University has successfully held the second medical conference at Jazeera Hotel on September 13, 2018. The main purpose of this conference is to present research findings on different topics in Health science.

Medical Professors, Doctors operating both in the country and outside, Health experts from International organizations, medical students and other honorable guests have attended the conference.

This year’s conference was rather different and more international as it is attended by two professors from Malaysia named Prof. Dato’ Dr. Razman Jarmin and Prof. Dato’ Dr. Zainul Rashid who operates at the Malaysian universities and the hospitals. In addition, Dr. Tareq Abdighalil from Egypt was with us who also operates at the Egyptian hospital in Mogadishu.

Medical doctors who are well known in the country have also participated at the conference and shared their knowledge with the attendees,

  1. Mohamed Mohamud Bidey
  2. Prof Osman Mohamud Dufle
  3. Mohamed Boodaaye
  4. Abdilhadi Salim
  5. Sheikh-don and others were the popular doctors who have attended the session,

During the conference, the researchers have presented studies on some diseases and medical conditions including:

  1. Novel Immunotherapy for Type I Diabetes by Professor Abdiaziz Sheikh Mohamood, professor of immunology at SIMAD’s school of medicine
  2. Hemorrhoids by Dr. Awale M. Abdullahi, a General surgery specialist
  3. Trauma patient Management by Prof. Dato’ Dr. Razman JarminConsultant General Surgery Hospital University Kepengsan Malaysia
  4. Breastfeeding by Yasin Abdirashid Adan, Medical Student At SIMAD University.
  5. Infertility by Prof. Dato’ Dr. Zainul Rashid DEPUTY DEAN (GRADUATE & INTERNATIONAL RELATIONS) Consultant Obstetrics and Gynecology Hospital University Kepengsan Malaysia
  6. Assessment of Risk Factors Associated with multidrug-resistant Tuberculosis in MDR-TB Treatment center by Abdirashid Moallim Hassan, Medical Student at SIMAD University.

This year, we have received generous sponsorship from the International Bank of Somalia and Mogadishu Stars Company.

However, SIMAD University is known to conducts numerous academic conferences in different areas including Business, Entrepreneurship, Technology, Engineering, Economics, and administration.

data.title: SU Hosts the 14th Convocation for the Class of 2018
data.content:
The 14th Convocation of SIMAD University was held at SIMAD University Main Campus on September 06, 2018. Member from SU board of trustees including Chairman of board Dr. Abdirahman Salah Maheilan, Deputy Chairman of the board Mr. Mohamed Al-qamis, and Director of Direct Aid office-Somalia and member of SU board Hassan Ahmed Badawi, were the guests of honor at the Convocation. 558 students have successfully finished their undergraduate degrees. The graduates have completed programs including Math and physics, Biochemistry, Statistics, Banking and Finance, Business Administration, Accounting, Information Technology, Computer Sciences, Public Administration, procurement and Logistics, Economics, Law, and Telecommunication Engineering.

SU Rector Ustad Dahir Hassan Arab has opened the ceremony and welcomed all the guests, He extended his thanksgiving to the graduation committee and SU staff at large and handed the microphone to Dr. Maheilan who conferred the scrolls to all the graduates.

This was followed by Deans’ call to the graduates, inviting them to come to the stage and then receive their graduation scrolls from Dr. Maheilan who is the chairman of the SU board of trustees. Receiving the scrolls from the chairman was the touching moment for the graduating students.

Dr. Abdirahman Salah Maheilan who spoke at the end of the ceremony congratulated to all graduates, he also confessed that he is happy to attend this graduation. “SIMAD University is one of the leading universities in East Africa and their first alumni have already made a positive impact on the society,” he said. He appreciated how the graduates and their management are connected and worked together during the journey and urged them to continue the good work.

For the first time, Students who completed the logistics and procurement department and Telecommunication Engineering department were among yesterday’s graduates.

In the end, the graduating students and their family and friends, as well as their lecturers have celebrated in the green campus of the university and took beautiful shots which roamed on social media.

SIMAD University always dedicated to offering excellent teaching facilities and research opportunities. It has produced competent and professional graduates who can develop the nation. Our dreams have not only become reality but simply yesterday’s graduates are a living proof and it is an indication that the university is moving forward. Furthermore, this graduation underscores our conviction that these graduates will go out and impact their world in more ways than we could have ever imagined, by applying their talents and overall development of the nation.

Congratulations to the class of 2018 for the mission accomplished.

data.title: SIMAD Attends 37th Round of Promoting Registrar and Admission.
data.content:
Dr. Ahmed Hassan, Dean of admission and record office of SIMAD University attended the 37th round of the Arab Collegiate Registrars and Admissions Officers Organization (ACRAO) which was held in Egypt and hosted by Zewail City of Science and Technology on 1-5 April 2018

This year, the round was titled “Registrars and Admissions between Globalization Courses and National Methodologies: Innovation and Development”.

Dr. Ahmed who spoke during the session shared with the participants about how SIMAD University practices the registrar and admission policies, in addition, he stated the university’s plans in the coming years about developing and improving the registrar and admission strategies of the university.

The conference was attended by more than thirty-five universities from Arab universities and delegations from Malaysia to exchange experiences and information on admission and registration and to establish a cooperative relationship with all the universities that participated in the conference.

During the summit, 25 research papers and workshops were presented. The conference's research was characterized by its evolutionary directives in the field of admission and registration in Arab universities, as well as the exchange of experiences and distinctive practices in the field of admission and registration.

SIMAD University has recently participated the 3rd Higher Education Congress and Eurasia Higher Education Summit in Turkey and signed a MoU with 9 Universities based in Turkey, in addition, the university has signed a MoU with the Vaal University of technology situated in South Africa on February 21, 2018 and This is another bold step to build strategic partnerships with the international universities and educational associations.

data.title: SIMAD Conducts Research Capacity Building Workshop for its Academic Staff.
data.content:
SIMAD University hosted two full-day workshop for the academic staff of the university on 2-3 April 2018. The workshop was co-organized by the Center for Research & Development and the Human Resource office, and it is facilitated by Professor Abdulkadir A. Egal who is the director of the center for sustainable livelihoods at the Vaal University of Technology in South Africa. This workshop is designed for academic researchers and junior faculty to provide increased capacity to produce high-quality publications and develop successful research center of excellence.

The Deputy Rector for institutional development, Eng. Mohamed Mohamud Mohamed together with Dr. Mohamed Ibrahim Nor, the deputy Rector for Academics opened the workshop and welcomed both the facilitator and the participants.

The first day of the workshop was about the research induction program and different topics were covered that include:

  • Research tools
  • Writing an effective research proposal
  • Importance of the research
  • Writing effective literature
  • Research skills that every researcher should have.  

In the second day, the workshop facilitator focused on applied research for institutional support and the main mission was to turn the first-day topics into an action and the establishment of research working groups and center of excellence. However, after long hours of analysis, the following recommendations were highlighted.

  • Establishing faculty research centers and faculty research committees.
  • To work upon the interdependence and collaboration of the faculties at the side of research.
  • Making all faculties of the university very strong and self-reliant.
  • Turning the knowledge into income generation and fundraising through research and community engagements.
  • Creating external funding for community research
  • Innovating SIMAD research style.
  • Establishing technical and center of excellence

In the end, the participants shared their views on the workshop, “this workshop encourages us to turn our knowledge into an action and be publishers” said Ayoub Mohamud Hussein who is the deputy dean, student affairs office and was among the beneficiaries of this workshop.

Finally, SU Rector Ustad Dahir Hasan Arab closed the two-day long workshop. He extended his thanksgiving to Professor Abdulkadir A. Egal for his time and sacrifice of coming to SIMAD and conducting such fruitful workshop. “In the coming years, delegates from SIMAD will travel to the Vaal University of Technology, and we are looking forward networking among the faculty deans and directors of both universities,” Said SU Rector Mr. Arab.

This workshop which was benefited by more than 50 staff was the beginning of efforts to maintain and turn into practice the joined forces agreement signed by SIMAD and Vaal on February 21, 2018.

data.title: SIMAD University hosts the 3rd Conference on Entrepreneurship and Business Development.
data.content:
The long-awaited annual conference on entrepreneurship and business development was held today at SIMAD University-main campus.

CEB is an academic conference where the researchers come together to discuss topics about the conference theme, this year’s theme was “Entrepreneurship: Prosperity for all” and more than 20 papers  were presented, some of the popular presented topics include:

1. Measuring service quality in the banking sector.

2. Challenges and success factors faced by Somali women Entrepreneurs in Mogadishu.

3. The effect of microfinance on poverty reduction.

4. Impact of petroleum exploration on Somali economy and its future implications.

5. Barriers to E-commerce development of SMEs in Mogadishu, Somalia, and more others.

SU Rector Ustad Hassan Arab officially opened the conference and welcomed the guests, he explained why SIMAD University always hosts such conference and the importance of the Entrepreneurship. “The Businesses and entrepreneurship activities were the only factors that survived the Somali community during the civil wars". He said.

Other top management of SIMAD also attended the conference including Eng. Mohamed Mohamud Mohamed and Abdiwali Mohamud Warsame.

Well-known Businessmen and entrepreneurs were the keynote speakers of the conference.

Abshir Abdi Dhore, general Director of Somali chamber of commerce, Mursal Abdullahi Kadiye, entrepreneur, and activist for the development of economic infrastructure, Mohamed Abdi Martello entrepreneurship expert, Abdikarim Mohamed Kariye, Operation manager of Hormuud telecom, Khadar Ismail, founder of Xadiid industries, Bright petroleum and director of IRise Hub, Mohamed Mohamud Shine, founder of Somali Online Market-SOOMAR, and Mohamed Farah Siyad long-time businessman were the keynote speakers of the conference.

During their speeches, they focused different topics include:

1. Impact of good governance on business development

2. Challenges facing Somali Business sector.

3. Possible opportunities for Somali Business sector.

4. Business and technology

5. Challenges and opportunities for Somali Online business and

6. Local products, organic food, and water.

During the conference, the best student paper award was succeeded by Mohamed Saney Dalmar and best lecturer paper award was succeeded by ustad Zakariye Ahmed nor Ali.

Dr. Ali Yasin Sheikh Ali senior lecturer of Entrepreneurship and research methods and Ustad Ali Ibrahim, dean, students’ affairs office of SIMAD chaired the parallel sessions of the conference.

Finally, we thank all the participants, guests, keynote speakers, and presenters who attended the conference, we hereby extend our appreciation to the presenters from Jamhuria University and our media partner Goobjoog business.

data.title: SIMAD University hosts the first medical conference in Mogadishu.
data.content:
SIMAD University held today the first medical conference in Mogadishu. Doctors, researchers, medical professors, medical students, practitioners, health officers from the ministry of health and other distinguished guests participated at the conference. The conference was held at Jazeera Hotel in Mogadishu.

SU rector, Dahir Hassan Arab together with Nasra Ahmed, the director of research and development center of SIMAD opened the conference and welcomed all the guests.

During the conference, it was focused three pressing health issues that currently the Somali communities are struggling with. The surge in cancer cases, the antibiotic resistance crisis, and Obstetric and gynecologic malpractices in Somalia were the addressed subject matter during all the sessions.

Dr. Mohamed Yusuf, Chairman of Somali Medical Association and Director of Madina Hospital is addressed the conference and suggested to establish the “Somali medical journal, which all the medical pieces of writing will be published, he also suggested finding the solutions of Somali health crisis through research and publications.

Distinguished guest Abdirashid Khalif, director of The Heritage Institute addressed the conference and suggested to establish youth associations and pressure groups to prevent any medical falsifications done by either government or doctors. “The medical issue is something collective, so it should be focused together," Abdirashid said.

Dr. Ali Bashi Omar, Ophthalmologist and President of Somali Ophthalmologist Association also addressed to the forum and remarked that many Somali people die due to the wrong prescriptions. "Our Country should have a national plan to control the crisis of antibiotic resistance in our community" Dr. Ali Bashi added.

Professor Abdiaziz Sheikh Mohamud, Lecturer of immunology at SIMAD university suggested a presentation about "the crisis of an antibiotic resistance"  and chaired a session on the same topic,  during this session Prof Abiaziz and the participated doctors suggested insights about how the misuse of antibiotics deteriorates the diseases, they put forward to make National Antibiotic Prescription plan is important to avoid the misuse of penicillin.

Dr. Zahra Awad, obstetrician and gynecologist at Al-Zahra Specialty Hospital was also given a talk to the conference and focused “the OBGYN Malpractices and Wrong perceptions In Somalia versus the Correct OBGYN practices” she concluded that “A healthy child can come from a healthy Mom so that the mothers should take balanced diet, and vitamin pills during the pregnancy and breastfeeding time”

After panel discussions and questions from the participants to the panelists, finally, the Dean of the faculty of medicine at SIMAD University together with the SU rector Ustad Dahir Hassan Arab concluded the event and again thanked all honored and distinguished guests of the conference.

SIMAD University promised that this conference will be annual based, and the next conference will have papers.

data.title: SIMAD University Celebrates 18 years of fountain knowledge and wisdom.
data.content:
A well-organized ceremony for the commemoration of the 18th anniversary of SIMAD University was held today at the green campus of the university, it was the first time of the university gardens hosted such event, the anniversary board gets on in the ground and the red carpet for the 2017 award winners was prepared diligently.

SU Senate, director of Direct Aid-Somalia, all staff, parents, students, Alumni, and other respected guests attended the occasion, the ceremony started with holy Koran verses followed by fun moments including lucky draw and tug of war.

Sheikh Hassan Ahmed Badawi, director of direct Aid-Somalia opened the session and prolonged the rich history of SIMAD University, and how it was established. He said “we embed our service of education with food so as to motivate the people to join the education centers” he thanked Direct Aid –Somalia ex Africa Muslim agency which was the organization funded and founded by this great university of SIMAD and many schools in Somalia, “we experienced one year that no Somali child goes to school and that was really disaster” he added, “we established SIMAD because of the need for the higher education at that time, and we are grateful now the 18 years of constant development” he concluded.

SU Rector Dahir Hassan Arab addressed the event, and drag out the next years’ plans, he mentioned that the university will have all the faculty labs in 2020, and student service center block which now under maintenance will officially launched in January 2018, he gives to his prayers to the dead employee of SIMAD, “we miss precious employee during our struggle of making this nation great who contributed to the university, so that we will never forget and our hearts will pray for them eternally” he added. He also praised all the staff of the University for their Tireless Collaboration to the university, particularly the award winners of 2017, “I congratulate to award winners of 2017, especially the females which hold and manages the backbone offices of the university, for sure the girls are more trustful when it comes the responsibility” he concluded

data.title: OUR FOUNDING FATHERS
data.content:
Every institution that exists has its builders who established it in the first place. SIMAD is not an exception. Therefore, it is worth mentioning the role of individuals who have managed to bring out into existence this tremendous change. These public figures share similar characteristics of vision, humbleness, courage, and ambition.

H, E Hassan Sheik Mohamoud, Former President of the Federal Republic of Somalia and the first Dean of SIMAD, the Founding father of the institute who, among others, has worked hard to set the strong foundation for the curriculum. He initiated a culture of transparency and created strong solidarity among the staff through merit and competence that has led the popular slogan of “SIMAD Family”.

During his term in Office, honorable Hassan Sheikh had foreseen the potential need of SIMAD for resources mainly human capital. Therefore, the authorities sent so far more than 80 recipients of a scholarship for postgraduate studies. The sponsored ones have returned and they have taken part in enhancing the overall development of SIMAD; at the same time sending a strong signal that SIMAD is a place of hard work, where talents are created.

Many norms and internal culture have been formed, in the meantime while addressing the individual. “Ustaad” for instance is widely used across the campus right now, a culture of respect and gratitude that always expresses appreciation and humbleness while serving as a team.

To make sure that his legacy does not go in vain, honorable Ustaad Hassan Sheikh instilled in the minds of SIMAD Family that this institution must exist forever and hence continuously go forward with concrete success and development. He repeatedly used to say that if SIMAD fails to exist, it is shame on me to say I used to work in SIMAD before. Among the habits that SU Family inherited from Ustaad Hassan are the culture of reading, learning, and teaching.

In his semester address to the students and staff, he used to stress that SIMAD Family and campus are peaceful and no conflict and fighting cannot take place on the campus. Since the inception of SIMAD, no conflict/violence among students and lecturers have taken place. This important achievement led by Ustaad Hassan came through setting high moral values and not stiff and strict punishments.

Ustaad Hassan left an enduring culture, shared values, vision and solidarity among SIMAD Family members that will remain forever. His stances, decisions, talks, slogans, and examples are and always will be an inspiration to all SU Family.

Farah Sheikh Abdikadir, the Former Director of Africa Muslims Agency (AMA) Office in Somalia, has been a long-serving individual who has worked hard to ensure that secondary school graduates achieve higher education at home.

From 1992 to 1999, he was working in humanitarian and helping the needy people. This assistance included providing primary and secondary education. Post-collapse of the government, the people were desperate and were not even thinking about tertiary education. However, early 1997 to 1999, the ambition of higher education came back due to the vast number of students who completed their secondary schools and were not able to study abroad.

So, Ustaad Farah had an idea that worth devoting resources; money, human and time. This idea was establishing a higher learning institute in Mogadishu.

Ustaad Farah looked similarly minded educators and found Ustaad Dhobale and Ustaad Hassan Sheikh. Proposals and plans were prepared and approved by African Muslims Agency Authority to get funding for such a noble project.

The idea turned into an institution that its reputation crossed the borders and is known all over the world. The noble characters of Ustaad Farah will remain in SIMAD. You are a role model to all SIMAD Family.

Mohamed Hussein Dhobale was one of the founders of SIMAD. He has dedicated all his time and energy to establishing SIMAD. He believed that he could serve the Somali people at a time most of the educators fled the country for their own safety. He had the dream and vision that one day an institution like SIMAD can exist amid the anarchy and chaos.

He was critical of the corrupted practices of all kinds. He inculcated to SIMAD students that knowledge without ethics and good character will destroy the person and that knowledge and skills gained from SIMAD are a double-edged sword. He used to share with students that the man who studied accounting at SIDAM to be able to embezzle the corporate and NGOs money and funds, who unfortunately realized that accounting teaches effective internal control and not how to commit fraud.

Ustad Dhobale served a number of positions at SIMAD. Remarkably, he served as the academic director. He also helped the establishment of similar institutions in Galka’yo. He was the consultant to the founders of SIMPA in Galka’yo.

He was a writer in the local newspapers and at the same time used to recite poems of the Somali poets. He used to strive to find job opportunities for SIMAD graduates by matching the opportunities and well-qualified graduates. He used to follow-up until the process is completed.

He was a man who is in control of his ego and humble for both students and staff. He used to sit with students and discuss critically and allow them to express their opinion freely. His objective was to eliminate the mental picture of dictatorship management of students and teach them to accept the opponents’ opinions.

Thank you for reading.

data.title: SIMAD UNIVERSITY: CELEBRATING 17th ANNIVERSARY
data.content:
A colorful occasion commemorating the 17th Anniversary of SIMAD University was held at the Library hall in SIMAD University Main Campus. The event was attended by top management of SIMAD, Director of Direct Aid, SIMAD University lecturers, alumni, parents, students and other honorable guests. All came together to engage, celebrate and reflect the magnificent milestones achieved by the University.

Ustad Yusuf Moallim Ahmed narrated first the history of SIMAD University from 1999 and the complex challenges the university has triumphed over.

“On 6th November 1999, it was the day SIMAD University was established. We have gone through a rough time. We have managed to work under a war whereby some of our students & staff lost their lives, but we had always a positive impulsion and determined vision to serve our community”, Yusuf Moallim Said.

The university was established as an institute of higher education on November 6, 1999, and later in 2011 was upgraded into a fully fledged university. During that time, the University has made enormous growth and developments and it is still going forward to achieve more.

Each year, the university celebrates SIMAD DAY, the day the university was established. And today marks the 17th anniversary of SIMAD University. We all had come together to reflect the success and achievements of the university. Also during the occasion, the university’s top performing staffs in this year were rewarded. (See below to see the list of award recipients).

Finally, the Rector of SIMAD University conveyed heartfelt congratulations to all SU staffs, alumni, students and other honorable members on the 17th Anniversary of the university.

“Without them, we would not have come together today and celebrate these achievements”, Said by the Rector.

He also paid his attributes to the top performing staffs in this year and encouraged them to keep up their feat. The Rector also shared where the university is going to be in the next years.

“Where do we go then? SIMAD University was one of the first institutions to have offered Information Technology in Somalia. Therefore, we’re planning to focus on two main things: Research and Information Technology. On the day the university was established, we’ve taken a responsibility of becoming a knowledge distributor and creator. And knowledge creator can only be achieved through making a culture of research”, The Rector said.

He also added that the university is planning to reform the infrastructure of the faculties. Every faculty will have a firm infrastructure, resource subjects, and other facilities. In addition, new faculties of sciences will be soon offered.
